violations.description: Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitize solution for mechanical warewashing machine was not maintained at the proper concentration. > Dishwashing unit did not contain any sanitizing solution when measured.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Different species of raw animal food was not properly separated from each other or arranged according to minimum cooking temperatures. > Upright refrigerator contained beef raw beef products over raw fish products.--Equipment/utensil was not properly cleaned and sanitized between uses to prevent cross-contamination. > Discovered spoon in out dated, molded sour cream container.--Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. > Under counter salad fridge contained raw beef over ready to eat biscuits.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. > Undercounter prep cooler by salad prep station cannot maintain a temperature at or below 41 F. Average temperature measured close to 50 F.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. > Discovered many different homemade sauces in refrigerator dated from the months prior that had not been discarded after ten days.
Contaminated food disposition. — --Contaminated ready-to-eat food was not discarded as required. > Discovered whole tomatoes in the walk in cooler that were moldy and caved in.

General equipment and utensils - Materials and design. — --Material of nonfood contact surface was not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent and cleanable. > Basement shelving units are raw wood material and need to be sealed/painted over to provide a smooth, nonabsorbent, easily cleanable surface.

Clean-in-place (CIP) equipment - Design and construction. — --Food contact surface of clean in place equipment did not meet proper design and/or construction characteristics. > Found that many refrigeration and prep units had damaged gasket seals that need repaired/replaced.

Sanitizing solution testing devices. — --Test strip or kit that accurately measures the sanitizing concentration was not provided. > No test strips were provided for measuring the concentration of sanitizers.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was reused or used more than one time. > Cardboard being used as a shelf liner in the back dry storage area with mop sink. Cardboard should not be used as a shelf liner.

Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. > All 3 compartment sinks were not properly air gapped. > Ice machine in the back room is not properly air gapped. > Bar ice holder is not properly air gapped.
